content.explanation:
**About To — Immediate Future (अभी होने वाला है!):**

"About to" = अभी-अभी / बस अभी होने वाला है (happening very very soon!)

🇮🇳 ट्रेन अभी चलने वाली है।
🇬🇧 The train **is about to** leave.

🇮🇳 मैं अभी खाना खाने वाला हूँ।
🇬🇧 I **am about to** eat.

**Structure:** Subject + am/is/are + **about to** + V1

**About To vs Going To:**
| About To | Going To |
|----------|----------|
| बस अभी (seconds/minutes) | Future में (hours/days) |
| "I am about to call" (dialing now!) | "I am going to call later" (plan) |
| Very immediate | Not necessarily immediate |

**Negative (was/were just about to = पर रोक दिया):**
🇮🇳 मैं जाने ही वाला था कि बारिश शुरू हो गई।
🇬🇧 I **was about to** leave when it started raining.
rules: Am/Is/Are + about to + V1 = something happening in seconds/minutes
I → am about to; He/She/It → is about to; We/You/They → are about to
Past: was/were + about to (something was imminent but may have been interrupted)
Negative: am/is/are NOT about to + V1 (= definitely not happening soon)
About to = most immediate future (more urgent than "going to")
Common in announcements: "The flight is about to depart."
Interrupted: "I was about to call when..." = something stopped it
content.memoryTrick: **ABOUT TO = LAUNCHING 🚀** — जैसे rocket launch countdown में 3...2...1...🚀 — About To बताता है वो last second वाला moment। 'Going to' = next week; 'About to' = 3...2...1!

examples.hindi: Film शुरू होने वाली है — जल्दी बैठो।
examples.english: The movie is about to start — sit down quickly.

examples.hindi: मैं अभी call करने वाला हूँ।
हम खाने वाले ही थे कि phone बजा।
examples.english: We were about to eat when the phone rang.
The meeting is about to begin.

examples.hindi: वह अपना पासवर्ड भूलने ही वाला था।
examples.english: He was about to forget his password.

mistakes.why: About to + V1 (base form, not past tense)

mistakes.wrong: The train is going to leave now.
mistakes.correct: The train is about to leave.
mistakes.why: 'About to' for immediate seconds — 'going to' for future plans

speakingTips: Announcements में: 'Attention please, the bus is about to depart.'
Dramatic stories: 'I was about to sign the contract when I noticed an error.'
Daily use: 'I am about to head out — do you need anything?'

vocabulary.example: The doctor will see you shortly.
An impending storm is about to hit.
I was about to speak when he interrupted me.
The principal is about to announce the results.

alternatives: i am just about to go.
practice.hint: about to + base verb = just going to

practice.english: The train is about to leave.
alternatives: the train is just about to depart.
she is on the verge of tears.

practice.hindi: फिल्म अभी शुरू होने वाली है।
practice.english: The movie is about to start.
alternatives: the film is just about to begin.

practice.hindi: मैं अभी खाना खाने वाला हूँ।
practice.english: I am about to have my food.
alternatives: i am just about to eat.
he was on the verge of speaking.

practice.hindi: मीटिंग अभी खत्म होने वाली है।
practice.english: The meeting is about to end.
alternatives: the meeting is almost over.

practice.hindi: मैं अभी-अभी तुम्हें फोन करने वाला था।
practice.english: I was about to call you.
alternatives: i was just about to phone you.

practice.english: The baby is about to sleep.
alternatives: the baby is almost asleep.

practice.english: The sun is about to set.
alternatives: sunset is about to happen.

practice.hindi: मेरी बैटरी अभी खत्म होने वाली है।
practice.english: My battery is about to die.
alternatives: my phone battery is about to run out.

practice.hindi: वह जाने वाला था तभी फोन आया।
practice.english: He was about to leave when the phone rang.
alternatives: just as he was about to go, the phone rang.
practice.hint: was about to + leave + when + Simple Past

practice.hindi: क्या खाना अभी तैयार होने वाला है?
practice.english: Is the food about to be ready?
alternatives: is the meal almost ready?
practice.hint: Is + noun + about to + be + adjective ?

practice.english: The shop is about to close.
alternatives: the store is about to shut.

practice.hindi: मैं यह काम खत्म करने ही वाला हूँ।
practice.english: I am just about to finish this work.
alternatives: i am almost done with this work.
i am just about to go.
